details.description: Acipimox is predicted to increase the risk of rhabdomyolysis when given with Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises caution.
Both Atorvastatin and Acitretin can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.
Alcohol -induced liver disease increases the risk of hepatotoxicity in those taking Atorvastatin. Manufacturer makes no recommendation.
Both Atorvastatin and Alectinib can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.
Atorvastatin slightly to moderately increases the exposure to Aliskiren. Manufacturer makes no recommendation.
Both Atorvastatin and Anidulafungin can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.
Apalutamide is predicted to decrease the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises monitor.
Aprepitant is predicted to increase the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises monitor and adjust dose.
Both Atorvastatin and Asparaginase can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.
Atazanavir is predicted to increase the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises avoid or adjust dose and monitor rhabdomyolysis.
Belumosudil is predicted to increase the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises avoid or adjust dose.
Both Atorvastatin and Bexarotene can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.
Bezafibrate increases the risk of rhabdomyolysis when given with Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises monitor.
Bosentan is predicted to decrease the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer makes no recommendation.
Bulevirtide is predicted to increase the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises avoid or monitor.
Cabazitaxel is predicted to affect the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises take 12 hours before or 3 hours after Cabazitaxel.
Both Atorvastatin and Capecitabine can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.
Carbamazepine is predicted to decrease the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises monitor and adjust dose.
Both Atorvastatin and Carmustine can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.
Both Atorvastatin and Caspofungin can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.
Ceftobiprole is predicted to increase the concentration of Atorvastatin. Manufacturer makes no recommendation.
Cenobamate is predicted to decrease the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer makes no recommendation.
Ceritinib is predicted to increase the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises avoid or adjust dose and monitor rhabdomyolysis.
Ciclosporin markedly to very markedly increases the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises avoid or adjust Atorvastatin dose.
Cilostazol is predicted to increase the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises caution.
Ciprofibrate increases the risk of rhabdomyolysis when given with Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises avoid or adjust dose.
Clarithromycin is predicted to increase the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises avoid or adjust dose and monitor rhabdomyolysis.
Both Atorvastatin and Clofarabine can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.
Cobicistat is predicted to increase the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises avoid or adjust dose and monitor rhabdomyolysis.
Colchicine has been reported to cause rhabdomyolysis when given with Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises caution or monitor.
Both Atorvastatin and Crisantaspase can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.
Crizotinib is predicted to increase the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises monitor and adjust dose.
Both Atorvastatin and Cytarabine can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.
Dabrafenib is predicted to decrease the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer makes no recommendation.
Danicopan is predicted to increase the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises caution.
Atorvastatin is predicted to increase the risk of rhabdomyolysis when given with Daptomycin. Manufacturer advises monitor.

Darunavir is predicted to increase the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises avoid or adjust dose and monitor rhabdomyolysis.
Both Atorvastatin and Decitabine can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.
Both Atorvastatin and Doxycycline can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.
Dronedarone slightly increases the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises monitor and adjust dose.
Efavirenz is predicted to decrease the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer makes no recommendation.
Elafibranor might increase the risk of muscle effects when given with Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises monitor creatine kinase.
Eltrombopag is predicted to increase the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises monitor and adjust dose.

Enzalutamide is predicted to decrease the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer makes no recommendation.
Both Atorvastatin and Eravacycline can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.

Etravirine is predicted to decrease the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer makes no recommendation.
Fenofibrate increases the risk of rhabdomyolysis when given with Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises monitor and adjust Fenofibrate dose.

Both Atorvastatin and Fluconazole can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.
Both Atorvastatin and Fluorouracil can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.
Both Atorvastatin and Flutamide can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.
Fosamprenavir is predicted to increase the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises avoid or adjust dose and monitor rhabdomyolysis.
Fostamatinib is predicted to increase the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises monitor and adjust dose.
Fostemsavir is predicted to increase the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises adjust starting dose and monitor.
Both Atorvastatin and Gemcitabine can increase the risk of hepatotoxicity.
